rationale: Both candidates miss the two sharpest GT bugs (the abort slot-leak and the cumulative/delta chunk_np re-slice bug) and the latent ceil-division bug; neither is a strong recall performer. Y independently surfaces the legacy-checkpoint/v2-tokenizer collision (matching GT's second High-severity finding via load_weights fragility/inferred_channels heuristic) and gives more nuanced, cross-referenced analysis (sibling function inconsistency, dead-code consumer check), though its confident claim that slot-leak cleanup is 'handled' is likely a false reassurance on a real bug. X's findings (unused sampling params, missing try/except, test-coverage gaps) are grounded but generic and don't overlap with any GT concern. Both are well-structured with file:line citations and concrete fixes/commands, so actionability is comparably high.
